Sandbox_Storm_ServerFps: Server FPS (tick rate + lockFps + physics FPS)
Sandbox_Storm_ServerFps_tooltip: Unified server FPS. Drives the main-loop tick gate (1000/fps ms), PerformanceSettings.getLockFPS() on the server, and the IsoPhysicsObject.update() FPS scalar — all three together. 10 = vanilla 10 TPS. Higher = faster simulation but more CPU.
Sandbox_Storm_AnimalLOSTickInterval_tool: Per-animal stride for IsoAnimal.updateLOS(). 1 = vanilla every-tick. Larger = each animal scans LOS every Nth tick (cheaper). 0 disables LOS entirely.
Sandbox_Storm_ZombieCullThreshold_toolti: Storm-controlled cull threshold. 500 = vanilla cap (default). Larger = allow more live zombies before culling. 0 disables culling entirely (no zombies ever queued for deletion).
Sandbox_Storm_ServerLosThreads_tooltip: Number of concurrent player LOS scans per tick. 1 = vanilla single-threaded baseline. Up to 16. The helper pool always pre-allocates 15 threads regardless; this knob only controls how many receive work each tick.
Sandbox_Storm_ChunkRecalcThreads_tooltip: Number of background workers draining ServerChunkLoader's recalc queue (cell-edge wiring after async chunk load). 1 = vanilla single worker. Up to 16. The pool always pre-allocates all 16 workers regardless of this value (1 vanilla + 15 Storm extras); the knob only controls how many receive work (the rest park at toThread.take). Extras share the same toThread/fromThread queues; each holds its own GetSquare. Cuts main-thread freeze when several cells finish loading in the same batch. Adjacent cells processed concurrently is rare but possible — boundary squares are not locked.
Sandbox_Storm_PreloadChunkOnRecalc_toolt: EXPERIMENTAL. When on, IsoChunk.doLoadGridsquare (vehicle/zombie/corpse/blood spawn, grass regrowth) runs on the ServerChunkLoader recalc worker before the cell reaches the main thread. RecalcAll2 then skips per-chunk doLoadGridsquare on main, cutting the worst-case freeze. Touches global singletons (VirtualZombieManager, VehiclesDB2, IsoWorld.currentCell) from a non-main thread — may race with concurrent main-thread work. Default off.
